title: 48-hour miracle: How Delhi just did something it hasn't done in 3 years
content: Delhi's air quality reached the 'good' category for the first time in nearly three years. Continuous monsoon rains washed pollutants away, lowering the Air Quality Index significantly. This event highlights how quickly weather can cleanse the city's atmosphere. However, maintaining this improvement remains a significant environmental challenge for Delhi. Experts emphasize that year-round emission reduction policies are crucial for sustained clean air.

title: Kerala medical student killed over 'forced conversion' claims in Uzbekistan
content: A Kerala medical student was murdered in Uzbekistan by a fellow student. The victim allegedly faced repeated demands to convert her religion before her death. Uzbek authorities have arrested the accused student in connection with the ongoing investigation. Indian authorities are assisting the victim's family and liaising with Uzbek officials. A second post-mortem was conducted in Kerala after the body's repatriation.

title: PM Modi gifts Steve Waugh rare framed photo during MCG visit
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited the Melbourne Cricket Ground with Australian PM Anthony Albanese, gifting Steve Waugh a framed photo from their meeting nearly 20 years ago. The leaders unveiled a sports cooperation roadmap and backed stronger sporting ties. Albanese also announced Chennai will host the first-ever Big Bash League match outside Australia during the 2026-27 season.

title: 4 burnt alive in car, 7 die fleeing on foot in deadly Spain wildfire
content: At least eleven people died after a wildfire swept through southern Spain overnight.  Authorities reported four victims were found inside a vehicle near the mountains.  Seven others died after abandoning their cars and attempting to flee on foot.  Spain has long grappled with devastating wildfires exacerbated by strong winds and heat.  The nation experienced record-breaking heat in June, contributing to excess deaths.

title: Hours after return to Twitter, Zuckerberg gets business proposal from product head
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced the new Muse Spark 1.1 AI model on X.  X's Head of Product Nikita Bier then offered Creator Monetization for his account.  This exchange occurred on Elon Musk's platform after Zuckerberg's AI announcement.  The Muse Spark 1.1 model is designed for coding and complex AI tasks.  Developers can now access this powerful new AI through Meta's API.

title: 'Tastes like cough syrup': Telangana techie's wife sent text hours before death in US
content: A husband faces murder charges after his wife's death was initially reported as sudden. Raajitha Sabbineni's last message about a bitter smoothie became key evidence. Autopsy results revealed she died from strangulation, contradicting the initial account. Investigators uncovered evidence of the husband's alleged affair with another woman. He was arrested in Bellevue, Washington, four months after their wedding.

title: Reverse oil flow: India supplies gasoline to Russia - why it matters
content: Indian refiners are well equipped from a product-quality standpoint to supply the gasoline grades required by Russia. However, Oil minister Hardeep Singh Puri has said that Indian companies are not directly selling gasoline to Russia.

title: 29-year-old Smriti Mandhana scripts history, becomes youngest ever to ...
content: Smriti Mandhana celebrated her 300th international appearance during India's historic women's Test at Lord's, becoming the youngest woman to reach the milestone. She reflected on overcoming doubts after the 2017 World Cup and said Lord's was the perfect venue for the occasion. Mandhana also stressed moving on from setbacks and returning to basics while preparing for red-ball cricket.

title: Melinda Gates to students: When a woman steps into power, it is better for all
content: Melinda French Gates received the Josef Korbel Award for her philanthropic work. She stressed that empowering women builds stronger families and nations. French Gates highlighted the importance of centering women in philanthropic efforts. Her organization Pivotal invests in entrepreneurs and advocates for paid leave. She urged students to contribute their skills to local communities.

title: Kazakh prince bought one of New Jersey’s priciest homes for just $10
content: A New Jersey mansion valued at $35.5 million was reportedly sold for just ten dollars.  This luxury estate was transferred to Daniyar Kessikbayev, who has political ties.  Kessikbayev is married to the daughter of former Malaysian Prime Minister Najib Razak.  The property's ownership has drawn scrutiny due to these significant political connections.  This unusual transaction is part of a pattern of symbolic property deals.

title: Elon Musk slept in office, showered at YMCA and coded 7 days a week
content: Elon Musk's journey to remarkable wealth was marked by immense challenges and unwavering commitment. He was known for his grueling 100-hour work weeks, often sleeping in his office and utilizing YMCA facilities for basic hygiene. Early projects like Zip2 and X. com provided the groundwork for his later achievements. Despite facing financial ruin in 2008, Musk's relentless spirit triumphed, exemplified by the eventual success of SpaceX, earning him admiration worldwide.

title: Heat of Europe's AC ban comes to New York; Mayor Mamdani shares AC tips
content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani urged residents to reduce electricity use. He asked people to adjust AC settings and limit unnecessary power consumption. The city is also implementing similar measures in government buildings. Officials aim to reduce pressure on the power grid during the heat wave. This appeal comes as electricity demand rises across New York City.

title: China's mega dam on Brahmaputra facing major natural threat: Report
content: The warning comes at a time when Beijing's massive dam project has already become a geopolitical flashpoint. India has repeatedly expressed concerns over China's upstream activities on the Brahmaputra, citing potential risks to water security, ecology and downstream communities. The latest study now raises fresh questions over whether the project also carries significant geological risks acknowledged by Chinese scientists themselves.

title: 'Polygamy practitioners to lose govt jobs': Assam's budget proposes strict rules
content: Assam's government proposed barring polygamous individuals from state welfare schemes.  Government employees practicing polygamy may face dismissal from their service.  The state also plans to amend existing rules for disciplinary actions.  This initiative aims to promote women's empowerment and gender justice.  The government will resume its welfare schemes from August onwards.

title: 'We will definitely try to sort it out': BCCI to review Team India after England tour ends
content: The Board of Control for Cricket in India will review the men's T20I team's performance. This detailed review will commence immediately after the ongoing England tour concludes. The team has faced significant challenges during their recent white-ball matches. Problems will be identified and addressed following the series' conclusion. Team India aims to rectify issues and improve future team performances.

title: Engineering student got no Wi-Fi, bad food in hostel; court orders Rs 30K refund
content: A district consumer commission directed a Greater Noida hostel to refund Rs 30,000. The student paid an advance for accommodation and promised facilities. The hostel failed to provide good food and working Wi-Fi. The commission found this a deficiency in service under the Consumer Protection Act. The hostel must also pay interest, compensation, and litigation costs.

title: Beyond building homes: How Odisha is combining affordable housing with rooftop solar
content: Odisha is revolutionizing housing schemes by incorporating rooftop solar systems, allowing beneficiaries to acquire subsidized solar panels for their new homes. This initiative is designed to lower long-term electricity costs for low-income families, making housing more affordable. Additionally, it encourages the adoption of clean energy, thus broadening the benefits of green housing to a greater segment of the population.

title: 'I'll do everything in my power to ... ': Salah reacts to Egypt's World Cup exit
content: Egypt captain Mohamed Salah shared an emotional message with fans after their World Cup exit. He promised to do everything in his power for Egyptian football's international success. The Pharaohs achieved a historic World Cup journey, topping their qualifying group. They secured their first knockout victory against Australia in a penalty shootout. Their dream run concluded with a narrow defeat against Argentina in the Round of 16.

title: 'Critical 3rd parties': UK puts Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Oracle under oversight
content: Major cloud providers like Microsoft and Google are now critical third parties.  This designation brings their cloud operations under direct regulatory oversight.  The new framework aims to safeguard the country's financial system resilience.  This move addresses increasing reliance on cloud services by financial firms.  The regulations take effect on July thirteenth, enhancing financial stability.

title: Actor Rajpal Yadav gets 3 months jail as HC upholds conviction in cheque bounce cases
content: The Delhi High Court, led by Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma, has sentenced Bollywood actor Rajpal Yadav to three months of imprisonment in each of seven cheque bounce cases stemming from a Rs. 9 crore debt dispute. Rejecting any immediate relief for the actor after a failed settlement process, the court ordered Yadav to pay Rs. 1.05 crore to the complainant per case, alongside fine adjustments for his wife, Radha Yadav. Get the full breakdown of the legal ruling, fine distributions, and Rajpal Yadav's statements regarding his severe financial setbacks here.

title: 15 killed in Philippines landslides as Typhoon Bavi wreaks havoc
content: Typhoon Bavi wreaked havoc in the Philippines, leading to tragic landslides that claimed the lives of at least fifteen people. The approaching storm caused significant evacuations and transport disruptions, as Taiwan braced for severe flooding and landslides, resulting in widespread school and business closures. Although weakening, the typhoon was poised to impact northern and eastern Taiwan before heading towards eastern China after affecting regions in Taiwan and Japan.

title: 1996 Srinagar mob violence: NIA files chargesheet against Shabir Ahmad, 5 Hurriyat leaders
content: The National Investigation Agency filed chargesheet against Shabir Ahmad Shah and five Hurriyat leaders. This case relates to a 1996 incident of mob violence and firing on police. The agency alleged leaders incited violence and raised secessionist slogans during a funeral procession. Evidence collected establishes their roles in the criminal conspiracy and unlawful assembly. The investigation into this 1996 incident is continuing.

title: Passenger nearly pulled out of Ryanair plane after cabin window breaks mid-flight
content: A Serbian tourist was nearly pulled from a Ryanair flight after a cabin window detached mid-air. Fellow passengers bravely pulled the man back inside the aircraft, which was returning to Thessaloniki. Oxygen masks deployed and a strong smell spread through the cabin following the decompression. The incident occurred over North Macedonia, possibly due to engine debris. Authorities are now investigating the cause of the window detachment.

title: India’s first hydrogen-powered train notified: Know route, launch date & what makes it special
content: The government says that project underscores Indian Railways' wider focus on technological innovation, improved energy efficiency and environmentally sustainable transportation, while supporting India's national objectives on clean energy and achieving net-zero carbon emissions.

title: 'We would pour extra water...': Ex-batting coach reveals Kohli's England preparation
content: Former batting coach Sanjay Bangar detailed Virat Kohli's intense preparation methods.  Kohli repeatedly practiced in Mumbai to simulate English swing and moisture.  This meticulous work helped Indian batsmen succeed in England previously.  The team now faces a challenging ODI series after a T20 disappointment.  Kohli and Rohit Sharma are expected to lead the batting attack.

title: 'Bones beginning to show': Sonam Wangchuk loses 7.5 kg amid hunger strike
content: Sonam Wangchuk stated his hunger stabilized after thirteen days of fasting at Jantar Mantar. He asserted his constitutional right to peaceful protest should not be violated by authorities. Wangchuk supports students demanding accountability for examination irregularities and pending Ladakh issues. He believes the education minister's resignation could restore youth confidence politically. The protest continues with a planned march to Parliament on July 20.

title: Ram Mandir donation case: Supreme Court to hear pleas seeking CBI probe on July 13
content: The Supreme Court will hear petitions on July 13 concerning alleged Ram temple donation embezzlement. Three petitions seek a court-monitored CBI probe into financial irregularities. Petitioners also demand a forensic audit of the Trust's entire finances. An investigation is underway in Uttar Pradesh, with eight accused arrested. Temple trust members express faith in the ongoing investigation and administration.

title: He quit Tech Mahindra to follow cinema; debut film earned Rs 40 crore
content: Former Tech Mahindra network engineer Sai Marthand has emerged as one of Telugu cinema’s breakout directors after his debut film Little Hearts became a surprise box office success in 2025. In a resurfaced Instagram post, he humorously recalled watching films and filmmaker interviews during his work-from-home days before quitting in 2023 to pursue filmmaking. The film’s success earned him industry recognition, and he is now preparing his next project with actor Adivi Sesh.

title: Rajesh Sharma's medical statement deleted as film body demands probe into insect bite incident
content: Rajesh Sharma remains hospitalised after an alleged insect bite on Prabhas' film set; his family's statement was deleted within 24 hours. AICWA demands a probe, medical cost coverage, and action against negligence. NDTV reports his breathlessness and blood sugar have stabilised, though he's not yet declared out of danger.

title: 'Cops could have alerted us ... I believed them': CM Vijay recalls Karur stampede
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay addressed a public meeting in Karur. He distributed compassionate appointment orders to 32 families affected by a stampede. The chief minister described the stampede as the most painful time of his life. He alleged that the Tamil Nadu Police had staged drama during the incident. This visit marked Vijay's first official trip to Karur since assuming office.

title: 'They offered namaz ...': CM Yogi slams SP, Cong for 'appeasement politics' amid Ayodhya row
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath accused opposition parties of appeasement politics. He alleged they disrespected Hindu faith by allowing prayers at Hanumangarhi temple. Adityanath stated these parties opposed Ayodhya's development and Ram temple construction. He highlighted the current government's role in building the grand Ram temple. The chief minister also mentioned opposition to Ayodhya's transformation and new projects.

title: 'It's really going badly': India coach puts onus on players to change mindset
content: India's assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate expressed disappointment with the team's overseas T20I results. The team has now lost consecutive bilateral T20I series against England and Ireland. Ten Doeschate emphasised the need for players to accept underachievement in foreign conditions. He urged the squad to focus on excelling in challenging overseas environments for future tournaments. India aims to avoid a whitewash in the final T20I against England.

title: India set to see 25% rise in petroleum product exports
content: Although India imports around 90% of its crude oil requirement, it has become one of the world's largest exporters of refined petroleum products by using its large and sophisticated refineries.

title: Delhi schools to conduct 'good touch, bad touch' sessions under POCSO awareness drive
content: Delhi schools will conduct good touch and bad touch awareness sessions this month. Child protection committees will be established in all schools by July's end. Teachers and staff will receive training on the POCSO Act and child safety protocols. The campaign involves police, counsellors, and anganwadi workers supporting the initiative. Students will also receive self-defence training and gender sensitisation sessions.

title: ‘Search my name’: Haaland's cheeky post as Google joins viral 'Viking Row' craze
content: Norway's Erling Haaland encouraged fans to search his name on Google. This action revealed an animated Viking Row celebration, a popular tradition. Haaland also revived his forgotten rap career with a new song remix. He has scored seven goals in the FIFA World Cup tournament. Norway now faces England in the quarter-finals after a historic win.

title: Bengaluru class 8 girl dies by suicide; death note cites ‘humiliation’ at school
content: A fourteen-year-old student died by suicide after alleged humiliation at her school. She left a note expressing distress over being blamed for something she did not do. The student had previously complained to the headmaster about unfair treatment by teachers. Authorities are investigating the death note and a separate complaint from other students. An assistant cook has been removed from her job following allegations of abusive behavior.

title: South Indian States, Central agencies to form permanent anti-drug coordination network
content: Mechanism involving police forces of southern Indian States of Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, along with the Union Territory of Puducherry will be established to strengthen anti-drug surveillance in the region

title: ‘Dhoom’-style burglar who targeted only malls held in Anantapur
content: Additional SP Syed Mahaboob Basha said Nagaraju allegedly broke into malls across three States over five years, avoiding homes so as not to rob families of their savings

title: Madras High Court restrains Election Commission from notifying byelections in five constituencies in Tamil Nadu
content: Chief Justices Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan issue the interim injunction till July 31, 2026, with respect to Tiruchi East, Perundurai, Ambasamudram, Viralimalai, and Karur constituencies.

title: Koli community demands mandatory registration, auditing of RSS
content: Led by community leader Lacchappa Jamadar, protestors in Kalaburagi back Home Minister Priyank Kharge’s push for transparency

title: Dry July 2026 ahead for Telangana as monsoon shifts elsewhere
content: Significant rainfall is likely only if weather systems develop closer to the Andhra Pradesh coast, says Telangana Development Planning Society (TGDPS) scientist

title: Vijay's Karur visit updates: T.N. Chief Minister announces memorial for stampede victims on behalf of TVK
content: The president of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, during his speech, termed the DMK the "vending machine" of corruption.

title: NC urges INDIA bloc leaders to join J&K’s demand for Statehood, constitutional guarantees
content: NC leader Tanvir Sadiq says all leaders should rise above party lines and stand united for the people of Jammu & Kashmir at the July 20 protest; BJP announces its own agenda with protests on employment issue on the same day

title: U.P. Governor asks women to be 'expert mothers' before becoming IAS officers, teachers
content: She urged young women to continue their education and careers after marriage while contributing to the well-being of their families and society

title: Mizoram battles rain fury; over 80 families moved to safer places
content: Landslides, rockfalls and other rain-related incidents have also been reported at more than 29 locations across the State, though no casualty or loss of life has been reported so far

title: Govt. amends rules to strengthen regulation of drug formulations with high alcohol content
content: High alcohol-containing formulations to require licences and to be dispensed only under prescription under Schedule H1 of the Drugs Rules, 1945

title: No free bus travel for women in Delhi without Pink Saheli smart card from August 1
content: From August 1, pink tickets shall be issued only to those women passengers who have a valid Pink Saheli smart card, which they need to tap at the time of boarding

title: Is there any bar on allowing outsiders in Cabinet meetings? | Explained
content: On July 4, del­eag­a­tions of the DMK, the AIADMK and the BJP met Gov­ernor Rajendra Vish­wanath Arlekar and sub­mit­ted sep­ar­ate memor­anda urging him to ini­ti­ate legal action against the par­ti­cip­a­tion of “two private indi­vidu­als” in a recently held Cab­inet meeting

title: Concerns arise over weak Dharani architecture allowing potential access to land data by private persons
content: Bhu Bharati still functions on the old architecture (of Dharani) which reportedly has weaknesses that give scope for change in land classification, extent and other details

title: PM Modi arrives in New Zealand on final leg of three-nation tour
content: During his two-day visit, PM Modi will meet with New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon and other leaders, focusing on enhancing economic and trade relations

title: Owaisi demands Permanent Residence Certificate to help Telangana electors in SIR process
content: Hyderabad MP says Karanataka issues PRC certificates through a government order under the Karnataka Sakala Services Act, 2011.

title: NIA files chargesheet against six separatist leaders in 1996 Kashmir mob violence case
content: The six were charged under provisions of the Ranbir Penal Code, 1989, for criminal conspiracy, attempt to murder, rioting and assault on public servants, along with Section 13 of the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967

title: Bengaluru man arrested for attempting to kill pregnant wife; remanded to judicial custody
content: The couple had reportedly been fighting frequently after learning about her pregnancy; disputes stemmed from domestic issues as well as arguments over Peer’s first marriage

title: Cheque-bounce cases: Delhi HC upholds Rajpal Yadav's conviction, sentences him to three months in jail
content: Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma also directed Rajpal Yadav to pay more than ₹1 crore to the complainant in each of the seven complaints

title: CJP protest day 21: Wangchuk says hunger has 'stabilised', warns against any attempt to remove him
content: When asked about the possibility of the administration intervening if his health deteriorated, Mr. Wangchuk said he was at the protest site voluntarily, and there was no reason for the authorities to remove him

title: Why is Chhattisgarh government asking farmers to adopt DSR method for paddy cultivation | Explained
content: Chhattisgarh’s DSR push comes amid rainfall uncertainty, water stress and labour shortages. A look at its benefits, challenges and adoption hurdles

title: Totapuri mango growers to get subsidy transparently, no favouritism: Chittoor Collector
content: Collector Sumit Kumar said eligible growers would be identified through gram sabha resolutions and crop e-KYC, and that the government had fixed a ₹4-a-kg support price for Totapuri mangoes

title: TVK government changes the name of free bus travel scheme for women
content: ‘Magalir Vidiyal Payanam’ has been renamed as ‘Magalir Payanam’. Metropolitan Transport Corporation buses sported ‘Magalir Payanam’ boards on Thursday. It’s an attempt to erase political identities, says Thangam Thennarasu

title: CPI(M) Thiruvananthapuram district leaders reject reports of internal differences, factionalism
content: At a joint press conference of top district leaders, CPI(M) district secretary V. Joy says fake narratives are being spread by some sections with ulterior motives to destroy the party and take public attention away from protests against Vizhinjam stake transfer

title: NGT directs authorities to keep sealed resort buildings shut in Segur elephant corridor
content: Observing that the legality of the disputed resorts is already before the Madras High Court, the Tribunal refrained from examining the demolition issue but ordered strict surveillance to protect the corridor

title: Business owners protest move to close Kalhatty Ghat Road to tourist vehicles
content: The Madras High Court recently passed orders directing that a status report be filed by the State government on why tourist traffic was permitted to ascend the Kalhatty Ghat Road, stating that the stretch must remain closed to tourists until further orders

title: E20 fuel may lead to 3-5% mileage hit in ‘some’ vehicles, Govt. admits as it defends blending
content: However, the government added that E20 fuel has a higher-octane rating, and provides several other vehicular and emission-related advantages over unblended fuel

title: Opposition on a mission to criticise Hindu traditions and Sanatan, says U.P. CM Yogi
content: The Chief Minister urged the citizens of the state to reject those with “negative mindsets” that oppose progress

title: Man hurls papers at Supreme Court Bench after incoherent submissions
content: Litigant-in-person escorted out after disruption in court; incident recalls past security breaches in the Supreme Court

title: BJP urges TVK govt. to address vacancies in State-run arts and science colleges
content: It was a bitter truth that students did not prefer State-run colleges that lacked qualified teachers, necessary buildings, modern labs, and basic amenities, Mr. Nagenthran said

title: Hooda-Surjewala mic exchange sparks off discussion on power dynamics in Haryana Congress
content: The timing of the exchange between the two leaders too matters. The party’s narrow loss in the 2024 Assembly polls has revived internal demands for change

title: Karur stampede: Madurai Bench of Madras HC permits T.N. government to conduct event to issue job appointment orders
content: The court, however, said the employment offer would be on a temporary basis, subject to judicial review

title: Science should reach society with integrity and transparency: Soumya Swaminathan
content: MSSRF Chairperson virtually addressed the IISc convocation; total of 1,452 PhD and Master’s students, and 118 undergraduate students received their degrees

title: Panipuri seller held with 650 grams of opium in Vijayawada
content: Acting on a tip-off, the Patamata police arrested Mukesh Jat near Bharati Nagar and seized 650 grams of opium

title: Football fans in Kerala transform their homes into colourful tributes to their teams
content: For these two fans, who live near Kochi, who have painted their houses in the colours of the countries they support in the FiFA World Cup, football is more than a seasonal obsession

title: Oman after Dubai: A new battle to bring Mahadev App kingpin back to India | Explained
content: With Indian investigating agencies trying to extradite Saurabh Chandrakar – the kingpin in the alleged Mahadev Online Book betting app scam – from Oman, the high -profile case is back in focus

title: Andhra Pradesh to showcase handlooms, textiles at Bharat Tex 2026 in Delhi
content: The expanded State pavilion at Bharat Mandapam from July 14 to 17 will feature textile parks, GI products and about 40 entrepreneurs from Champion and Aspirational districts

title: Vizianagaram to hold special SIR campaign on July 11, 12 ahead of deadline
content: About 15.90 lakh forms have been distributed and 75% uploaded; the district administration will hold a weekend drive before the July 14 deadline, with the draft roll due on July 21

title: Take up GST cut on commercial LPG with Centre, hoteliers ask Andhra government
content: A delegation of the Andhra Pradesh Star Hotels Association met Civil Supplies Minister Nadendla Manohar, seeking a lower GST on commercial LPG cylinders and the restoration of input tax credit for the sector

title: TTD suspends all special darshan formats as it gears up for twin Brahmotsavams
content: All departments have been directed to complete preparations well ahead of schedule to handle the expected surge in pilgrim footfall during the festivities commencing from September 8

title: What led to deaths after C-section deliveries in Kota hospitals | Explained
content: The discovery of the fake oxytocin injections has once again highlighted India’s very critical problem of drug quality and safety.

title: No privatisation of APSRTC, e-buses won’t transfer assets to private firms: government
content: The induction of electric buses under the Centre’s PM e-Bus Sewa Scheme does not involve the transfer of RTC assets or services to private entities, Special Chief Secretary (Transport) Krishna Babu has said

title: US firm CorroHealth closure in Kerala: No breakthrough in Minister-level meeting
content: Kerala govt issues ultimatum to medical coding firm to present alternative proposals to retain its workforce in the State or face prosecution for alleged violations of labour laws

title: Panel to be formed soon to examine liquor retail models in other States: T.N. Minister for Prohibition Vignesh
content: The committee will study the functioning of excise departments in Kerala, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and Odisha, including liquor procurement, retail operations, and employee service conditions, says K. Vignesh

title: Assam govt. tables ₹2,85,084 crore Budget for FY27
content: Presenting his maiden Budget, Assam Finance Minister Jayanta Malla Baruah also announced to continue all the major schemes rolled out during the last five years, while stating that he aims to cut Budget deficit to ₹419 crore

title: Lokesh proposes Andhra-Korea Investment Desk, invites Kia to expand near Visakhapatnam
content: On the fifth day of his South Korea tour, the Minister invited Kia to set up EV and export facilities near Visakhapatnam Port and sought Korean partners for the State’s data centre and renewable energy plans

title: Why is Gujarat betting  ₹6 lakh crore on data centres? | Explained
content: The State hopes to attract investments worth ₹6 lakh crore and build 7.5 GW of data centre capacity over the next few years, positioning itself alongside established hubs such as Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu and Telangana

title: Use hydel generation only to meet peak hour demand, official directs AP-Genco
content: He directed it to maximise thermal power generation by operating all available units at full capacity, strengthening preventive maintenance and minimising outages

title: Kozhikode railway station in Kerala restores train services after clock tower collapse
content: Debris clearance and demolition work continue at the site following the structural failure during monsoon rains

title: Leaders voicing their opinion were unique to Congress: Punjab in-charge Baghel
content: “Unlike in other parties, it is not a one-way communication and command in the Congress. The Congress values and respects the seniority and contribution of each leader, which is acknowledged and rewarded at the right time,” says Bhupesh Baghel

title: Opinion: Blog | Amaira, And The Daily Goodbye Every Parent Takes For Granted
content: I kept thinking about that moment every parent knows so well. The one where you watch your child disappear through the school gate, believing they will come home.

title: Hackers Exploit Software Flaw To Steal Rs 7.34 Crore From Gujarat Bank
content: CID officials said the hackers exploited a flaw in the banking software, which they suspect may have affected 14 to 15 other banks across the country.

title: Seeking Stamina Boosters, Thousands Fall Into Cyber Extortion Trap
content: Operating through sophisticated call centers, the gang exploited personal vulnerabilities by selling fake stamina-enhancing medicines online and then extorted the buyers by posing as law enforcement officials.

title: Head And Shoulders Outside: Man Nearly Sucked Out Of Flight As Window Detaches
content: The passenger was a tourist from Serbia who was travelling from Thessaloniki in Greece to Memmingen in Germany. He was hospitalised with friction burns but was otherwise in good condition.

title: E20 Fuel Can't Be Cheaper Than Pure Petrol. Centre Explains Why
content: The country's ethanol march began with a pilot project in 2001 and a formal policy in 2013. However, blending hovered at a mere 1.5 per cent before 2014 as production relied solely on seasonal sugarcane crops

title: 2 Killed, 2 Injured As Truck Rams Vehicle On UP Expressway
content: Circle Officer Rajeev Kumar Sharma said the DCM (registration number UP 81 GT 6248), which was carrying dates, was moving from Charkhari to Sambhal.

title: "Teacher Pretended Nobody Was Missing": Parents Of Girl Who Jumped To Death
content: Shivani Meena underlined that her daughter was very happy when she arrived at the school that November morning.

title: Petitioner Abuses Chief Justice, Throws Papers, Supreme Court Spares Him
content: The incident took place around 11 am before a partial working-day Bench of Justices KV Viswanathan and Alok Aradhe.

title: Change "Addictive Design" Or Face Heavy Fine: EU Warns Meta
content: Brussels accused the US tech giant of failing to limit the risks the platforms posed to users, especially children and vulnerable adults, because of features designed to keep them on Facebook and Instagram.

title: Meta's AI Glasses Could Reportedly Be Getting Creepier
content: Watching, listening, recording non-stop - Meta's next line of AI glasses could be a privacy nightmare right out of Black Mirror.

title: "Unhealed Wound": Vijay On Karur Stampede, Hands Over Jobs To Families
content: Forty-one people were killed in the incident. A Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) probe into the tragedy is currently underway.

title: Why India Is Focussing On Myanmar As Part Of Its Act East Policy
content: India is seeking to enhance its cooperation with Myanmar in line with our 'Act East' and 'Neighbourhood First' policies.

title: Opinion: Opinion | By Trump, For Trump: Inside The Murky Election Crisis America Is Sleepwalking Into
content: Trump has learnt that overturning an election after losing is extraordinarily difficult. This time, he is shaping the battlefield before America votes.

content: If rationalised, the deal can also add greater momentum to India's Make-in-India vision and further reinforce New Delhi as a credible and trusted defence partner in the region.

title: Pregnancy Norms Stall IPS Officer's Training Despite Favourable Court Opinion
content: The top court, however, expressed an oral agreement with Sengar's submissions against a 1993 Ministry of Home Affairs Office Memorandum (OM) governing maternity-related interruptions in IPS probationary training.

title: 'She's In Shock, Can't Sleep': Father Of Doctor Assaulted By Sena Corporator
content: Dr Srishti Baviskar and her colleague Dr Vaibhav Salunkhe are in a state of deep shock, Dr Srishti's father Mahendra Baviskar told reporters today

title: End Of Hustle Culture? Gen Z's 'Effort Recession' Hits 6 In 10 Indian Firms
content: According to the report, the trend is strongest in information technology, professional services, and construction, infrastructure and real estate.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Settles 827 Points Higher Amid Positive Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Brent crude traded near $76 while US West Texas Intermediate was below $72 per barrel.

title: Deepak Nandal: Wanted Gangster Whose Shooters Died In Gurugram Encounter
content: The Haryana Special Task Force (STF) has taken action against several members of the Deepak Nandal gang in recent months.
